content.problemStatement:
For a permutation XXX of length MMM,

let F(X,K)F(X,K)F(X,K) denote the number of arrays AAA of size KKK such that:


1‚Č§Ai‚Č§M1 \leq A_i \leq M1‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§M ;
Ai‚ąí1‚Č§AiA_{i-1} \leq A_{i}Ai‚ąí1‚Äč‚Č§Ai‚Äč for all 2‚Č§i‚Č§K2 \leq i \leq K2‚Č§i‚Č§K ;
min‚Ā°j=1A1Xj=min‚Ā°j=1AiXj\min\limits_{j = 1}^{A_1} X_j = \min\limits_{j = 1}^{A_i} X_jj=1minA1‚Äč‚ÄčXj‚Äč=j=1minAi‚Äč‚ÄčXj‚Äč for all 1‚Č§i‚Č§K 1 \leq i \leq K1‚Č§i‚Č§K
You are given three positive integers N,K,N, K,N,K, and VVV.

Find the sum of F(P,K)F(P,K)F(P,K) over all (N‚ąí1)!(N-1)!(N‚ąí1)! permutations of P=[1,2,‚Ä¶,N]P = [1,2,\ldots,N]P=[1,2,‚Ä¶,N] for which P1=VP_1 = VP1‚Äč=V.

Since the sum might be huge, please print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT, the number of test cases.
The only line of each test case contains three positive integers N,K,N, K,N,K, and VVV - the length of permutation PPP, the parameter KKK, and the value of P1P_1P1‚Äč.


Output Format
For each test case, output the required sum modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The only array possible is A=[1]A = [1]A=[1].
Test case 222: The only value of P=[2,1]P = [2, 1]P=[2,1].

Consider A=[1,1]A = [1, 1]A=[1,1]: min‚Ā°(P1)=min‚Ā°(P1)=2\min(P_1) = \min(P_1) = 2min(P1‚Äč)=min(P1‚Äč)=2
Consider A=[2,2]A = [2, 2]A=[2,2]: min‚Ā°(P1,P2)=min‚Ā°(P1,P2)=1\min(P_1, P_2) = \min(P_1, P_2) = 1min(P1‚Äč,P2‚Äč)=min(P1‚Äč,P2‚Äč)=1

Thus, there are two possible arrays AAA.
